fields.definition_origin: Pattern formalized 2023-2024 by Cal AI (Henry Langmack, Zach Yadegari) and Mau Baron (Prayer Lock) as the engineered narrative-driven loader that masks LLM latency at the AI plan reveal moment. Documented as an industry best practice in Adapty's 2026 'High-Performing Paywall' report — explicitly called out as the highest-converting compliant alternative to Apple's banned toggle paywall + a critical architectural element of long-onboarding success. Distinguished from the 'Wasted Loading Screen' antipattern (NN/g six-antipattern audit) which is the generic blank spinner — theatrical loading is *engineered* loading with narrative copy, brand-color animation, and a staged reveal. The 4-7 second loader specifically is the canonical 2026 pattern; <3s feels fake, >12s feels broken. Adapty 2026 documents loader-conversion-doubling effects vs blank-spinner equivalents.
fields.core_mechanism: The theatrical loader is the dramatic build-up that makes the AI plan reveal (item 12) feel earned. Mechanism is dual: (1) it MASKS LLM latency (~1-3s on GPT-4o p50, up to 8-12s p99) — without the loader, the user sees a blank spinner during the most leveraged moment of the app, killing the peak; (2) it COMPOUNDS sunk cost + IKEA Effect — the loader narrates the personalization computation ('Analyzing your responses', 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you', 'Building your plan') so the user feels their questionnaire labor is being processed in real-time. The loader is itself a Variable Reward (item 1 Hook) — anticipation peaks during the 4-7 seconds, ventral striatum dopamine fires (Knutson et al. 2001), and the reveal lands as resolution. Crucial timing: <3s feels too fast for AI to have computed anything (the reveal feels canned); >12s feels broken (user assumes app is hung); 4-7s is the sweet spot where the computation feels real but doesn't exhaust patience. The loader's copy must be specific to the user's questionnaire ('Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you') — generic 'Loading...' kills the effect.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Anticipation engages ventral striatum reward signaling (Knutson et al. 2001) — the loader IS the dopamine spike, the reveal is the resolution. The morphing copy stack engages reading-comprehension circuits (left inferior frontal gyrus) that keep the user mentally active rather than disengaged. Time-perception during engaging waits is reduced (Eagleman 2008 — engaged time feels shorter than blank time); a 7s engaged loader subjectively feels like 3-4s. Pre-attentive Gestalt grouping of brand-color animation makes the loader feel cohesive and intentional rather than broken.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Adapty 2026 'High-Performing Paywall' report explicitly documents the theatrical loader pattern as a top conversion lever: doubles trust + conversion vs blank-spinner equivalent (industry survey of paywall apps, n>500). (2) Cal AI architecture: 4-7s theatrical loader at step ~30 → full-bleed reveal → soft paywall → $30M ARR Year 1 (TechCrunch 2024). (3) Mau Baron Prayer Lock teardown: theatrical loader is named as a critical architectural element of the 5min→15min onboarding that tripled organic conversion (Starter Story full playbook). (4) Mobbin teardowns 2024-2025: Cal AI, Headway, Flo, Noom all use theatrical loaders before personalized reveals — pattern is universal across successful AI-wrapper apps. (5) NN/g six-antipattern audit explicitly distinguishes 'Wasted Loading Screens' (blank spinner during fetch) from engineered loading — the theatrical pattern is the antidote. (6) Eagleman (2008, 'Human time perception and its illusions') — engaged time feels shorter than blank time; theoretical foundation for why the loader works. (7) Counter-evidence: loaders that are too short (<3s) reduce conversion vs longer loaders — Adapty A/B test data shows 2s loader converts worse than 5s loader, even though 2s is objectively faster (the perception of computation matters more than literal time). (8) Loaders >12s (network-bound, no fallback) reduce conversion sharply — users assume app is hung and abandon.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Loader duration distribution (target 4-7s p50, p99 <12s with offline fallback), reveal-completion rate after loader, abandonment-during-loader rate (target <5%), perceived computation realness Likert (qualitative survey), paywall conversion rate post-loader+reveal vs blank-spinner+reveal control, LLM response p99 latency (informs max loader duration), offline-fallback trigger rate.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Adapty 2026: theatrical loader doubles trust + conversion vs blank-spinner equivalent (industry survey n>500). Cal AI / Mau Baron Prayer Lock public revenue trajectories implicitly validate the pattern at scale. RevenueCat 2025: AI-wrapper apps with engineered reveal+loader see +14% direct purchase lift. Specific A/B data: 2s loader vs 5s loader → 5s wins on conversion (perception > literal time). 12s+ loader → conversion craters (assumed hung).
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Implementation: Reanimated 3 fade-stack copy component with morphing strings ('Analyzing your responses' 1.5s → 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' 2.5s → 'Building your plan' 1.5s — total 5.5s). Brand-color animated arc / spinning loader (NOT generic iOS UIActivityIndicator), 80px diameter, 1200ms loop. Subtle pulse on background. Optional progress percentage 0-100% over 5.5s (artificial, but reinforces forward motion). (2) Min-duration enforcement: even if LLM responds in 1s, hold loader for min 4s — otherwise it feels fake. (3) Max-duration with fallback: if LLM call exceeds 10s, fall back to cached static plan + show reveal. Don't exceed 12s under any circumstance. (4) Streaming response: begin parsing LLM tokens as they arrive (Vercel AI SDK / OpenAI streaming) but don't render until min 4s elapsed; use the buffer to populate the reveal. (5) Copy specificity: each loader message references the user's questionnaire context ('Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' is generic-acceptable; 'Customizing for 30-minute sessions' references their specific answer). (6) Audio sting under 200ms when reveal lands (mute-respecting). (7) Haptic during loader: subtle .selection at each copy-stack transition; .success when reveal renders. (8) Offline-fallback architecture: if network drops during loader, gracefully transition to cached static plan template at 4s mark — never show error screen. (9) Pre-warm LLM call: dispatch the LLM request when user lands on step N-2 (one step before loader), so the actual loader duration is minimum perceived not minimum literal.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI step ~30 — 5-second theatrical loader with morphing copy ('Analyzing your responses' → 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' → 'Building your plan'), brand-orange spinning arc, soft pulse on background → full-bleed reveal of macro plan. Industry-canonical reference. (2) Mau Baron Prayer Lock — 5-7s loader narrating 'Crafting your personal prayer' / 'Drawing from sacred texts' → full-bleed AI prayer reveal. (3) Headway — 5-7s loader 'Curating your reading list' / 'Matching ideas to your goals' → personalized summary feed reveal. (4) Flo period tracker — multi-stage loader with health-data-themed copy → personalized cycle prediction reveal. (5) Noom — psychology-themed loader copy ('Building your behavioral profile') → personalized weight-loss plan reveal. (6) Anti-example: a generic 'Loading...' spinner with iOS UIActivityIndicator at the reveal moment — Wasted Loading antipattern; conversion craters vs theatrical equivalent. (7) Anti-example: a loader >12s without offline fallback — users abandon at 8-10s thinking app is hung. (8) Anti-example: loader copy that's identical for every user ('Computing...') — feels canned, kills the personalization perception.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary pattern. Full-bleed loader, haptic feedback at copy transitions, brand-color animation at 60fps, audio sting under 200ms — all mobile-native. Web equivalent: full-bleed loader page with morphing copy + animated SVG, audio optional (mute-respecting). Web doesn't have haptic equivalent. Mobile push notification on Day 1 referencing the named plan extends the theatrical-reveal narrative across days; web has no equivalent (outside email).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Strongly aligned with both 2025-2026 platform languages. Apple Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — translucent material + dynamic morphing make the loader feel premium and intentional, not stock. Material 3 Expressive (I/O 2025, 18k-participant) — its expressive shape system + spring motion are the substrate for the morphing copy stack and animated arc. Both platforms now offer expressive loader components (UIViewController.contentTransitionStyle, M3 LinearProgressIndicator with brand colors) — use them, don't fight them.
fields.category_fit: Strongest for: AI-wrapper (Cal AI, Prayer Lock, Headway, Blinkist, Speakly), wellness/habit AI (Cal AI, Flo, Noom), faith/lifestyle (Prayer Lock, Hallow), edtech (Headway, Blinkist). The pattern is fundamentally tied to AI plan reveal (item 12) — strongest where reveal is most leveraged. Less applicable: pure-utility (calculator), B2B productivity (no reveal), social (no plan to generate), fintech-utility (Acorns — link bank > everything). DO NOT use theatrical loader for routine in-app loads (would dilute the pattern + feel manipulative).
fields.tooling_options: Reanimated 3 + react-native-reanimated (fade-stack copy component, brand-color arc), react-native-skia (custom particle burst for reveal), Lottie (cheaper alternative for loader animation if Skia/Reanimated heavy), Vercel AI SDK (streaming LLM responses), OpenAI / Anthropic streaming API, Adapty (paywall placement post-reveal), Superwall (paywall A/B around loader+reveal). For audio sting: react-native-sound + brand-mastered audio file <200ms. For haptic: native sensoryFeedback API + VibrationEffect.
fields.ai_dependency: required: this pattern is fundamentally tied to LLM-generated reveal content (item 12). Without an LLM call, there's nothing to mask — the loader becomes pure theater (which still works, but loses the personalization edge). Required model class: GPT-4o / Claude Sonnet 4 frontier reasoning. Latency budget: 1-3s typical, plan for p99 8-12s with offline fallback.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to wire the loader as a 4-7s narrative-stack with min-duration + max-duration + offline fallback. Example fragment 1: 'Build the AIPlanRevealLoader component. Required: (a) min-duration 4s, max-duration 12s; (b) fade-stack copy component (Reanimated 3 timing(duration: 1500ms, easing: bezier)) showing 3 messages in sequence: \'Analyzing your responses\' → \'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you\' → \'Building your plan\' (each 1.5-2.5s); (c) brand-color spinning arc (80px diameter, 1200ms loop, react-native-skia or Reanimated SVG); (d) subtle pulse on background (Reanimated 3 sequence(scale 1 → 1.02 → 1, 2400ms loop)); (e) sensoryFeedback .selection on each copy-stack transition; (f) on LLM response, transition to reveal screen with .success haptic + 200ms audio sting. Forbid generic UIActivityIndicator. Forbid <3s loader duration. Forbid >12s without offline fallback.' Example fragment 2: 'Implement offline fallback: dispatch LLM call on step N-2 (one step before loader). If LLM hasn\'t responded by 10s into the loader, render cached static plan template (offline_plan.json bundled in app). Forbid showing a network-error screen at this critical moment.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told this is a TIME-BASED sequence, not a single frame. Example fragment 1: 'Generate THREE frames showing the theatrical loader sequence (each represents a 1.5-2.5s moment in a 5.5s total animation). Frame 1 (0-1.5s): full-bleed soft warm gradient background, centered brand-color spinning arc 80px diameter, copy below in 24pt Cabinet Grotesk Medium reading \'Analyzing your responses\'. Frame 2 (1.5-4s): same layout, copy crossfades to \'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you\'. Frame 3 (4-5.5s): same layout, copy crossfades to \'Building your plan\'. Subtle background pulse animation throughout. Forbid generic spinner without copy. Forbid Lucide / iOS system spinner.' Example fragment 2: 'Frame 4 (REVEAL — 5.5s+): full-bleed reveal of named personalized plan card with brand-color particle burst centered. The transition from Frame 3 to Frame 4 is a 350ms crossfade + scale-up. Forbid abrupt cut.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen defaults to: (1) iOS UIActivityIndicator / Material CircularProgressIndicator (generic, kills theater); (2) blank spinner with no copy (Wasted Loading antipattern); (3) loader <2s (LLM responded fast, no min-duration enforcement); (4) static text 'Loading...' instead of morphing copy stack; (5) no offline fallback (network drop = blank screen forever); (6) no haptic + audio integration; (7) Lucide sparkle icon as the loader animation (AI-slop tell). Counter-prompt: 'Build a theatrical loader, NOT a generic spinner. Required: 4-7s duration with morphing copy stack (\'Analyzing your responses\' → \'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you\' → \'Building your plan\'), brand-color animated arc (NOT iOS UIActivityIndicator, NOT Lucide), min-duration 4s enforced even if LLM responds faster, max-duration 12s with offline fallback to cached static plan, sensoryFeedback .selection at each copy transition, .success haptic + 200ms audio sting on reveal. Forbid generic spinners. Forbid blank loaders. Forbid <3s duration.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Generic blank spinner (Wasted Loading antipattern from NN/g six-antipattern audit). (2) Loader <3s (feels fake, too fast for AI to have computed). (3) Loader >12s without fallback (feels broken, user assumes app is hung). (4) Static 'Loading...' text instead of morphing copy stack (kills theater). (5) Loader copy identical for every user (kills personalization perception). (6) iOS UIActivityIndicator / Material spinner default (no brand identity, no theater). (7) Lucide sparkle / generic icon as loader animation (AI-slop tell). (8) No offline fallback (network drop = lost conversion). (9) Loader after every action (dilutes pattern; reserve for AI reveal moment only). (10) Haptic at every copy transition that's too aggressive (annoying — keep .selection subtle, .success only at reveal). (11) Audio sting too long (>500ms = obnoxious; cap at 200ms). (12) Theatrical loader BEFORE the questionnaire (no labor invested yet — feels manipulative).
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. (1) EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) and DSA flag deceptive personalization — the loader's copy must reflect actual computation happening, not fake activity. If the LLM call is mocked or pre-rendered, the loader becomes a dark pattern. (2) AI Act (EU, phased 2025-2027) requires transparency about AI-generated content; loader copy 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' is fine; 'Our PhD nutritionists are reviewing your data' would be deceptive. (3) Apple Guideline 4.0 — design must be polished; broken/excessive loaders have been cited. (4) FTC dark-pattern guidance — fake-progress indicators are flagged. (5) Mitigation: ensure loader copy is honest about what's happening (LLM is generating personalization), use 'Generated by AI' disclosure on reveal, don't claim human review unless true.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 4.0 — design polish. Apple HIG explicitly recommends 'clear loading indicators with informative copy'. Material 3 Expressive expressive motion guidance applies to loaders. Apple Privacy Manifest required for analytics + LLM-routing SDKs. Google Play target API 35 (deadline 2025-08-31) — runtime permissions discipline applies if loader needs network/storage perms.
fields.accessibility_implications: Loader must respect prefers-reduced-motion (replace spinning arc with static brand-color circle + crossfade copy stack). Sound on reveal must respect mute switch. VoiceOver/TalkBack must announce each copy-stack transition ('Analyzing your responses', 'Cross-referencing users like you', 'Building your plan') and announce 'Plan ready' at reveal. Dynamic-type to 200% must not break the loader copy layout. WCAG 2.2 §1.4.3 contrast 4.5:1 must hold against any background gradient. WCAG 2.2 §2.3.1 Three Flashes — animated arc must not exceed flash threshold. Loader must not block keyboard or assistive-tech navigation.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Loader duration calibration: too short feels fake, too long feels broken — A/B test 4s vs 5s vs 6s vs 7s to find your audience's sweet spot. (2) LLM latency variability: p99 GPT-4o responses can spike to 8-12s; max-duration enforcement + offline fallback is critical. (3) Cost: streaming LLM calls increase per-request cost ~10% over batch but improve perceived latency materially. (4) Cache strategy: identical-input plans should hash-cache for 30 days; high cache-hit rate after 1K users. (5) Network-flap recovery: backend must persist questionnaire state so reload mid-loader can resume. (6) Cultural calibration: theatrical pacing varies — US/Western markets tolerate 6-7s, JP/KR may prefer 4-5s, LATAM tolerates 7-8s with audio + warmth. (7) Performance on low-end Android: Reanimated 3 worklets handle 60fps fine on Pixel 6a class; older devices may drop frames — fall back to Lottie. (8) Copy localization: each language's word-length differs; layout must accommodate (CN/JP shorter, DE longer). (9) The loader IS the peak setup — if reveal fails, the loader's promise is broken; offline fallback must still feel personalized.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Pattern works cross-culturally with proper calibration. Loader duration sweet spot varies: US/Western 5-7s, JP/KR 4-5s (impatience higher with overt anticipation), LATAM 5-8s (tolerates warmth + audio), DACH 4-6s (data-grounded register). Copy must localize, not translate — 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' has equivalent register in EU/LATAM but feels invasive in JP/KR (try 'Carefully matching your answers'). Audio sting must match locale audio conventions (US bright synth, JP softer chime). RTL languages need mirrored arc rotation direction + copy alignment. Statistics in copy must localize ('2.4M users' is fine for global apps; for niche-locale apps, swap to local relevant numbers). Faith/medical reveals must respect cultural sensitivities.
fields.prescribed_action: Wire the AI plan reveal loader as a 4-7 second engineered narrative wait: morphing copy stack ('Analyzing your responses' → 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' → 'Building your plan'), brand-color spinning arc, subtle background pulse, sensoryFeedback .selection at each transition, .success haptic + 200ms audio sting at reveal. Enforce min-duration 4s + max-duration 12s with offline fallback to cached static plan. Forbid generic spinners, blank loaders, <3s duration, and Lucide sparkle / iOS system spinner defaults.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: The loader is the bridge between the final questionnaire question and the reveal — it must reference the questionnaire labor explicitly ('Analyzing your responses', 'Cross-referencing users like you'). Without sufficient questionnaire labor (10-15 minutes), the loader has nothing to dramatize and the pattern collapses into theater without substance.
fields.aha_relevance: The loader IS the dramatic build-up that makes the Aha (item 12 AI plan reveal) feel earned. It transforms LLM latency from a UX problem into a storytelling opportunity. Without the loader, the reveal lands flat (no anticipation peak); with the loader, the reveal lands as resolution to engineered tension. The loader is the most underrated element of the entire long-onboarding architecture.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Strongest with Magician (the loader IS the spell-casting moment, anticipation of transformation). Compatible with Sage (the loader narrates careful curation), Caregiver (the loader's warm copy reads as gentle preparation). Less natural for Outlaw (rule-breaking voice doesn't sit well with structured anticipation — adapt with rebel-coded copy 'Breaking the diet rules for you...'). Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Loading_States.json. Conflicts when archetype voice doesn't match loader register.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Wasted Loading Screens (the canonical antipattern this pattern solves); Generic Spinner (iOS UIActivityIndicator / Material default); Hollow Aha (loader without earned questionnaire substrate); Premature Authentication (loader before questionnaire = manipulative); Decorative Fake Progress (loader percentage that doesn't reflect actual computation).
